title: Makefile
documentation:
## Purpose
Carries Makefile source that defines targets, dependencies, and build recipes for the `make` utility. Makefiles are used across nearly every software project for compiling code, running tests, and automating repetitive tasks.

## When to use
Use `media:makefile;textable` when a pipeline node reads or generates a Makefile — for example, a build executor invoking `make`, a dependency graph extractor, or a code generation step that produces a project scaffold with a Makefile included.

## Notes
The primary filename `Makefile` (no extension) is not registered here; this type covers fragment files with the `.mk` extension that are included into a main Makefile. Tab characters are required for recipe lines — spaces are a syntax error. See also [Makefile](https://capdag.com/browse/media:makefile;textable).
